commit.message:
fix(soulmate-pack): moments paths + heartbeat intimacy gate + fresh-install signal + robustness (#96)

* fix(soulmate-pack): moments paths, heartbeat intimacy gate, stable fresh-install signal

- moments.py: store workspace-relative paths (artifacts/moments/...) instead of
  bare (voice/x.ogg) so the viewer works when served from any URL base, plus
  auto-migrate legacy bare entries on add/list/view. moments.html resolver
  prepends "/" for workspace-relative paths and falls back to /artifacts/moments/
  for legacy entries.
- should_message.py: gate action_type by intimacy level (voice >= L2, selfie >= L3,
  video >= L5). Forced --action above the gate downgrades to "message". Heartbeat
  no longer sends Level-4+ content at Level 0.
- HEARTBEAT.md: added per-level tone/content guidance so text actions also respect
  the relationship stage.
- AGENTS.md / HEARTBEAT.md: detect fresh install via data/intimacy.json absence
  instead of BOOTSTRAP.md, which gets overwritten by pack updates. BOOTSTRAP.md
  is now purely informational.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(soulmate-pack): trim HEARTBEAT/BOOTSTRAP/AGENTS for runtime tokens

HEARTBEAT.md is loaded every tick and AGENTS.md every turn, so verbosity
there is recurring cost. Compress the level-gate guidance to reference
sm-relationship instead of duplicating its table, drop meta-commentary
that's useful in the PR but not to the agent, and tighten code comments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(soulmate-pack): self-contained HEARTBEAT level guidance

The "see sm-relationship content gates" reference would force an extra
SKILL.md load at heartbeat time since step 2 only runs the script.
Inline the concrete L0-1 rules (which is the only thing heartbeat actually
needs — L2+ action types are already code-gated in should_message.py).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(soulmate-pack): collapse action gate dicts, drop redundant BOOTSTRAP.md

- merge _ACTION_MIN_LEVEL + _ACTION_WEIGHTS into single _ACTION_GATES tuple dict
- drop redundant "artifacts/moments/" entry in migrate skip-check
- delete BOOTSTRAP.md: AGENTS.md session-start already covers fresh-install

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): moments timestamps use user timezone

datetime.now() was naive, so isoformat() emitted no offset and browsers
rendered entries in whatever local zone they happened to be in. Read the
configured timezone from data/config.json (same pattern as
should_message.py) and use tz-aware datetimes throughout cmd_add + cmd_view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): moment captions must mirror conversation language

Agents default to English for meta text like captions unless told otherwise.
Make the language requirement explicit next to the moments.py call so a
Chinese chat produces Chinese captions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): harden HEARTBEAT silence rule + missing-skill bailout

Field report: an agent leaked prose into chat ("Good! send: true, action
is video. However, without the skills infrastructure available...").
Two failures — narrating instead of staying silent, and inventing
excuses when the target skill could not run.

Make the silence rule explicit with examples of forbidden output and
add "missing/unrunnable skill → HEARTBEAT_OK silent" to step 5 and the
sm-relationship step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "fix(soulmate-pack): harden HEARTBEAT silence rule + missing-skill bailout"

This reverts commit b86064d6b68cbc7a30163b4fe17784883382d4f8.

* fix(soulmate-pack): clarify HEARTBEAT silence rule covers send-true failures

Field report agent quote: "Good! send: true, action is video. However,
without the skills infrastructure available, I cannot execute... Let me
check if there's a fallback or if I should report HEARTBEAT_OK".

The immediate trigger (stale heartbeat instance on prod) is fixed by
redeploying, but the agent's narration shows the prompt was ambiguous:
HEARTBEAT_OK was never labeled as an internal stdout signal vs. chat,
and the send-true branch never said what to do if the target skill
cannot run. Both now explicit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(soulmate-pack): simplify HEARTBEAT — one header rule, terser steps

Fold per-step HEARTBEAT_OK echoes into a single "any failure/skip → HEARTBEAT_OK"
clause, sharpen the silence rule to "exactly one thing: chat OR HEARTBEAT_OK".
~30% fewer tokens at runtime, same guarantees.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(soulmate-pack): tighten HEARTBEAT silence rule

~80 words → ~30. Same load-bearing pieces: output = one of two, any
fail/skip → HEARTBEAT_OK, no meta-prose (with the examples that actually
stop agents from writing them).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(soulmate-pack): drop redundant forbidden-prose examples from HEARTBEAT

"Output ONE thing: X or Y" already excludes narration, JSON echoes,
and meta-prose. The example list was belt-and-suspenders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): restore HEARTBEAT_OK token in skip steps (not "done.")

openclaw heartbeat-events-filter matches replies beginning with the
literal HEARTBEAT_TOKEN ("HEARTBEAT_OK") to classify them as acks.
"done" would bypass the filter and leak to chat. Steps 1 and 3 now say
"→ HEARTBEAT_OK" explicitly, matching openclaw src/auto-reply/tokens.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): drop broken {baseDir} template in SKILL.md

Nothing in openclaw or pi-coding-agent substitutes {baseDir}. The agent
saw literal {baseDir}/scripts/intimacy.py and guessed skills/sm-relationship
(old pre-.agents/ convention), producing wrong paths in prod.

openclaw's skills prompt already instructs: "When a skill file references
a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory." So plain
scripts/intimacy.py works; the {baseDir} prefix was noise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(soulmate-pack): restore clarity in HEARTBEAT + AGENTS

HEARTBEAT step 5: `message|question` → sm-empathy read as a parallel
skill call (like sm-voice/sm-photo/sm-video), but sm-empathy is a tone
guidance skill, not a generation skill. Restore "text (tone via
sm-empathy)" — the agent composes text itself, consulting sm-empathy
for tone.

AGENTS step 3 (Post moment): "Caption in the conversation's language
(mirror user — do NOT translate to English)" said the same thing three
ways. Trim to "Caption in user's language — do NOT translate to
English" — one positive rule + one specific prohibition targeting the
real failure mode (English-default captions).

Applied to both top-level and sm-onboarding template copies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(soulmate-pack): caption in conversation's language, not user's

"User's language" means user's native/preferred language (persona default);
"conversation's language" means whatever language the current chat is
actually in. AGENTS.md Language rule says mirror the user when they
switch — captions should follow the live chat, not the persona default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): address PR review findings on moments + sentinel

Two independent reviewers flagged overlapping issues; this consolidates
fixes in one commit.

**moments.py — migration was unsafe**

- `list`/`view` persisted migrated index.json from a stale in-memory
  snapshot. A concurrent `add` writing between the read and save would
  be silently dropped (last-writer-wins). Make list/view read-only;
  rename `_load_and_migrate` → `_view_entries`; only `add` (single
  writer) persists. (Codex HIGH)
- `_migrate_bare_paths` assumed every entry is a dict and every media
  field a string. A malformed index (null entry, int field, partially
  migrated shape) crashed add/list/view. Added isinstance guards so
  non-dict entries and non-string values are skipped intact rather
  than mutated. (Codex HIGH)
- Migrator would also corrupt any future `http(s)://` media URL to
  `artifacts/moments/https://…`. Added a URL guard (`"://" in value`
  → skip). (Claude MED)
- `cmd_list`/`cmd_view` sort crashed on non-dict entries (e.g. stray
  `null`, string). Filter by isinstance before sorting.

**moments.html — resolver corrupted absolute URLs**

- `toRelPath` had no http(s) early-return, so a remote URL was
  rewritten to `/artifacts/moments/https://…`. Added a leading
  `^https?://` match. (Claude MED)

**should_message.py — negative/malformed level broke silence rule**

- `_pick_action` filtered `_ACTION_GATES` by level, giving an empty
  dict when `level < 0`; `random.choices([])` then raised IndexError
  on the heartbeat path, emitting a Python traceback instead of
  `HEARTBEAT_OK` (violates the silence rule). Clamp level to `>= 0`
  in `_read_context` and wrap the `int(...)` parse with a fallback
  so a malformed intimacy.json ("level": "oops") also normalizes to
  0 rather than crashing. (Claude MED)

**AGENTS.md + HEARTBEAT.md — fresh-install sentinel was unstable**

- PR #96 had switched the fresh-install signal from `BOOTSTRAP.md`
  presence (overwritten by pack updates) to `data/intimacy.json`
  absence. But intimacy.json is mutable runtime state: deletion,
  corruption, or a mid-provision crash all re-trigger onboarding on
  an existing user. (Codex MED × 2, Claude MED)
- Introduce `data/.onboarded` — a stable, write-once sentinel written
  LAST in provision.py (after SOUL.md, config.json, intimacy.json,
  MEMORY.md, dirs, assets). Survives pack updates (lives under
  data/); a crashed provision leaves no stale flag; later edits or
  deletion of intimacy.json no longer retrigger onboarding.
- Session Start rule (AGENTS.md step 1) + heartbeat gate
  (HEARTBEAT.md step 1) both key off `data/.onboarded` now.
- `reset.py` DATA_FILES list now includes `.onboarded` so `/init`
  clears it.

Verified via smoke tests in /tmp: negative level, "oops" level,
null/int/URL entries in index.json, reset.py + provision.py round-trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): atomic index write + broader URI guard + upgrade path

Round-2 review (codex) surfaced three issues on 2aaedb7:

1. **Atomic index.json writes** — save_index now writes to .tmp and
   os.replace's into place so concurrent list/view readers never see a
   torn file (which load_index would treat as []).

2. **Broader URI scheme guard** — migrator and moments.html toRelPath
   previously only skipped http(s):// (and "://" substring). data:,
   blob:, file:, etc. would be corrupted. Both now match any RFC-3986
   scheme prefix [a-z][a-z0-9+.-]*:.

3. **Upgrade path for pre-sentinel installs** — AGENTS.md and HEARTBEAT.md
   step 1 now require BOTH data/.onboarded AND data/config.json to be
   missing for fresh-install routing; an install that predates the
   sentinel (has config.json but no .onboarded) self-heals via touch
   instead of being re-onboarded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): unique tmp name + tighten pre-sentinel self-heal

Round-3 review follow-ups:

1. save_index now uses tempfile.mkstemp for a per-call unique tmp name
   so overlapping writers can't stomp each other's partially-written tmp
   and publish it via os.replace. Orphan tmp files are cleaned on error.

2. Upgrade self-heal now requires BOTH config.json AND intimacy.json
   present before touching .onboarded. This avoids auto-healing a
   partial-provision crash state (where config.json was written but
   subsequent steps failed) into "looks onboarded".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): atomic writes for intimacy.json + next_send + config

Round-4 review flagged three other shared-state writers using the same
non-atomic pattern that round-3 fixed in moments.save_index. Most
load-bearing: intimacy.py — the new pre-sentinel self-heal now depends
on intimacy.json existing AND being well-formed, so a torn read (empty
JSON → fallback to defaults) would silently drop user state.

- intimacy.save: tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace with orphan cleanup
- should_message._persist_next_send: same pattern (unique tmp per heartbeat)
- moments.cmd_set_profile: routes config.json write through the shared
  _atomic_write_json helper (extracted from save_index)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): explicit utf-8 encoding in atomic json writers

_atomic_write_json dropped the encoding="utf-8" that cmd_set_profile
previously passed via write_text — on non-UTF-8 locales (LC_ALL=C) this
would mis-encode CJK/emoji in config.json and intimacy.json. All three
atomic writers now pass encoding="utf-8" to os.fdopen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): explicit utf-8 encoding on json readers too

Mirror the writer-side encoding="utf-8" fix on the reader side. Under
LC_ALL=C (non-UTF-8 locale), read_text() uses ascii and
json.loads() would raise ValueError on CJK/emoji content — silently
swallowed by the except blocks in load_index, _user_tz, _read_json,
_load_next_send_at, and intimacy.load. Result: moments.json could
degrade to [] on next add (dropping history), config→{} (UTC/default
frequency), intimacy→defaults (losing streak/score).

All JSON read_text calls and the write_memory_md .md writer now pass
encoding="utf-8" explicitly.

Verified via LC_ALL=C roundtrip with CJK + emoji captions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): encoding=utf-8 on provision.py reads + writes

Last locale-dependent sites in this PR's scope. provision.py is already
touched for the .onboarded sentinel; finishing the UTF-8 hardening here
so CJK/emoji in persona name/bio/interests survive LC_ALL=C installs.

- reads: SOUL.md template, user.md parser, config.json fallback read
- writes: SOUL.md, config.json, intimacy.json, MEMORY.md, .onboarded

Verified: provision.py with a CJK persona argument completes cleanly
under LC_ALL=C LANG=C and produces well-formed SOUL.md / config.json /
intimacy.json with CJK content intact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(soulmate-pack): trim restated-mechanism comments on atomic writes

Simplify review flagged three comments that restated what tempfile.mkstemp
+ os.replace already make obvious. Keeping only the non-obvious WHY on
each site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ning Hu <ning@gensmo.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): atomic index write + broader URI guard + upgrade path

Round-2 review (codex) surfaced three issues on 2aaedb7:

1. **Atomic index.json writes** — save_index now writes to .tmp and
   os.replace's into place so concurrent list/view readers never see a
   torn file (which load_index would treat as []).

2. **Broader URI scheme guard** — migrator and moments.html toRelPath

   blob:, file:, etc. would be corrupted. Both now match any RFC-3986

3. **Upgrade path for pre-sentinel installs** — AGENTS.md and HEARTBEAT.md
   step 1 now require BOTH data/.onboarded AND data/config.json to be
   missing for fresh-install routing; an install that predates the
   sentinel (has config.json but no .onboarded) self-heals via touch
   instead of being re-onboarded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): unique tmp name + tighten pre-sentinel self-heal

Round-3 review follow-ups:

1. save_index now uses tempfile.mkstemp for a per-call unique tmp name
   so overlapping writers can't stomp each other's partially-written tmp
   and publish it via os.replace. Orphan tmp files are cleaned on error.

2. Upgrade self-heal now requires BOTH config.json AND intimacy.json
   present before touching .onboarded. This avoids auto-healing a
   partial-provision crash state (where config.json was written but
   subsequent steps failed) into "looks onboarded".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): atomic writes for intimacy.json + next_send + config

Round-4 review flagged three other shared-state writers using the same
non-atomic pattern that round-3 fixed in moments.save_index. Most
load-bearing: intimacy.py — the new pre-sentinel self-heal now depends
on intimacy.json existing AND being well-formed, so a torn read (empty
JSON → fallback to defaults) would silently drop user state.

- intimacy.save: tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace with orphan cleanup
- should_message._persist_next_send: same pattern (unique tmp per heartbeat)
- moments.cmd_set_profile: routes config.json write through the shared
  _atomic_write_json helper (extracted from save_index)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): explicit utf-8 encoding in atomic json writers

_atomic_write_json dropped the encoding="utf-8" that cmd_set_profile
previously passed via write_text — on non-UTF-8 locales (LC_ALL=C) this
would mis-encode CJK/emoji in config.json and intimacy.json. All three
atomic writers now pass encoding="utf-8" to os.fdopen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): explicit utf-8 encoding on json readers too

Mirror the writer-side encoding="utf-8" fix on the reader side. Under
LC_ALL=C (non-UTF-8 locale), read_text() uses ascii and
json.loads() would raise ValueError on CJK/emoji content — silently
swallowed by the except blocks in load_index, _user_tz, _read_json,
_load_next_send_at, and intimacy.load. Result: moments.json could
degrade to [] on next add (dropping history), config→{} (UTC/default
frequency), intimacy→defaults (losing streak/score).

All JSON read_text calls and the write_memory_md .md writer now pass
encoding="utf-8" explicitly.

Verified via LC_ALL=C roundtrip with CJK + emoji captions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(soulmate-pack): encoding=utf-8 on provision.py reads + writes

Last locale-dependent sites in this PR's scope. provision.py is already
touched for the .onboarded sentinel; finishing the UTF-8 hardening here
so CJK/emoji in persona name/bio/interests survive LC_ALL=C installs.

- reads: SOUL.md template, user.md parser, config.json fallback read
- writes: SOUL.md, config.json, intimacy.json, MEMORY.md, .onboarded

Verified: provision.py with a CJK persona argument completes cleanly
under LC_ALL=C LANG=C and produces well-formed SOUL.md / config.json /
intimacy.json with CJK content intact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(soulmate-pack): trim restated-mechanism comments on atomic writes

Simplify review flagged three comments that restated what tempfile.mkstemp
+ os.replace already make obvious. Keeping only the non-obvious WHY on
each site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ning Hu <ning@gensmo.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: drop broken {baseDir} placeholder from all SKILL.md files (#98)

* fix: drop broken {baseDir} placeholder from all SKILL.md files

Nothing in openclaw or pi-coding-agent substitutes {baseDir}. Agents saw
the literal token and had to guess what directory it meant. In prod this
produced wrong paths (e.g. `skills/sm-relationship/scripts/intimacy.py`
instead of the correct `.agents/skills/sm-relationship/scripts/intimacy.py`).

openclaw's skills prompt already instructs: "When a skill file references
a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory." So plain
relative paths work correctly. The {baseDir}/ prefix was pure noise
across the monorepo.

Also updates agent-studio's skill-design.md which was actively documenting
{baseDir} as the recommended pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(stylist): correct publish-html-page sibling path (1 up, not 2)

publish-html-page lives at .agents/skills/publish-html-page/, so from a
sibling skill like smart-wardrobe or weekly-planner the path is
../publish-html-page/, not ../../publish-html-page/ (which would resolve
to .agents/publish-html-page/ — doesn't exist).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tarot): drop {skillsDir} placeholder in decision-spread

Same bug class as {baseDir}: nothing substitutes {skillsDir}, agents guess
the older skills/<name>/ convention, which is wrong under .agents/skills/.

daily-pull is a sibling skill, so ../daily-pull/ is the correct path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ning Hu <ning@gensmo.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
